questions.scenario: You just finished a Slice → Waxing Slice → Infernal Slice combo.
questions.question: How much Soul Gauge did you gain from this combo?
questions.explanation: The basic combo (Slice → Waxing Slice → Infernal Slice) grants 10 Soul Gauge upon completion of Infernal Slice.

questions.scenario: Soul Slice has 2 charges. You have 40 Soul Gauge.
questions.question: Should you use Soul Slice?
options: Yes - use on cooldown to avoid overcapping
No - wait until Soul Gauge is at 0
questions.explanation: Soul Slice grants 50 Soul Gauge per use. At 40 Soul, using it won't overcap (90 max). Use charges on cooldown to maximize gauge generation.

questions.scenario: Death's Design has 5 seconds remaining on the boss.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: Death's Design (+10% damage) should never fall off. Refresh it with Shadow of Death when it's getting low to maintain the damage buff.

questions.scenario: Death's Design is at 55 seconds duration on the boss.
questions.question: Should you use Shadow of Death?
questions.explanation: Death's Design caps at 60 seconds. At 55s, using Shadow of Death would only add 5s, wasting most of the extension. Wait until it's lower.

questions.scenario: You have 95 Soul Gauge and both Soul Slice charges are available.
questions.question: What should you do?
options: Spend Soul first with Blood Stalk
questions.explanation: At 95 Soul, using Soul Slice (50 gauge) would overcap. Spend Soul first with Blood Stalk (50 cost) to make room for Soul Slice generation.

questions.scenario: You have Soul Reaver active and are at the boss's side (flank).
questions.question: Which ability should you use for the positional bonus?
questions.explanation: Gibbet has a flank positional. Use it from the boss's side for bonus potency.

questions.scenario: You're standing behind the boss (rear) with Soul Reaver active.
questions.question: Which ability should you use?
questions.explanation: Gallows has a rear positional. Use it from behind the boss for bonus potency.

questions.scenario: You just used Gibbet. What buff did you receive?
questions.question: Which Enhanced buff do you now have?
questions.explanation: Gibbet grants Enhanced Gallows. The next Gallows you use will deal bonus damage.

questions.scenario: You have 50 Soul Gauge. Gluttony is available.
questions.question: How many Soul Reaver stacks does Gluttony grant?
questions.explanation: Gluttony grants 2 Soul Reaver stacks, making it more efficient than Blood Stalk (1 stack) for the same Soul cost.

questions.scenario: A mechanic forces you to stand in front of the boss. You have Soul Reaver and True North available.
questions.question: What should you do?
options: Use True North and do positional
questions.explanation: True North allows you to ignore positional requirements. Use it when mechanics prevent proper positioning.

questions.scenario: You use Gibbet to finish a Soul Reaver stack.
questions.question: How much Shroud Gauge do you gain?
questions.explanation: Each Soul Reaver ability (Gibbet, Gallows, or Guillotine) grants 10 Shroud Gauge.

questions.scenario: You have 50 Shroud Gauge.
questions.question: What can you do with this Shroud?
questions.explanation: Enshroud requires 50 Shroud Gauge to activate. At 50 Shroud, you can enter your burst state.

questions.scenario: You have 2 Soul Reaver stacks and 4 enemies nearby.
questions.question: Which Soul Reaver ability should you use?
questions.explanation: Guillotine is the AoE Soul Reaver ability. At 3+ targets, it deals more total damage than single-target options.

questions.scenario: You have 90 Shroud Gauge. Blood Stalk is available.
questions.question: What should you prioritize?
options: Use Blood Stalk for Soul Reaver
questions.explanation: At 90 Shroud, you're close to capping (100 max). Enter Enshroud to spend Shroud before gaining more from Soul Reaver.

questions.scenario: You use Guillotine on a pack of enemies.
questions.question: What buff do you receive?
questions.explanation: Guillotine grants both Enhanced Gibbet and Enhanced Gallows buffs, allowing you to use either enhanced version afterward.

questions.scenario: You just entered Enshroud.
questions.question: How many Lemure Shroud stacks do you start with?
questions.explanation: Enshroud grants exactly 5 Lemure Shroud stacks, which you consume with Void/Cross Reaping.

questions.scenario: You used Void Reaping during Enshroud.
questions.question: What do you gain besides damage?
questions.explanation: Each Void Reaping and Cross Reaping grants 1 Void Shroud stack, which is used for Lemure's Slice oGCDs.

questions.scenario: You just used Void Reaping during Enshroud.
questions.question: What should you use next for maximum damage?
questions.explanation: Void Reaping grants Enhanced Cross Reaping. Alternate between them for the Enhanced damage bonus.

questions.scenario: You're in Enshroud with 5 enemies grouped together.
questions.question: Which GCD should you use?
questions.explanation: Grim Reaping is the AoE version of Void/Cross Reaping. Use it at 3+ targets during Enshroud.

questions.scenario: You're in Enshroud with 2 Lemure Shroud remaining.
questions.question: How many more GCDs can you use before Communio?
questions.explanation: Communio should be used when you have 1 Lemure Shroud remaining. At 2 stacks, use one more Void/Cross Reaping first.

questions.scenario: You have 1 Lemure Shroud stack remaining during Enshroud.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: Communio should be used when you have 1 Lemure Shroud remaining. It's your high-potency finisher.

questions.scenario: You just used Communio.
questions.question: What proc did you receive?
questions.explanation: Communio grants Perfectio Ready, allowing you to use the powerful Perfectio follow-up attack.

questions.scenario: You have 4 Void Shroud stacks during Enshroud.
questions.question: How many Lemure's Slice can you use?
questions.explanation: Lemure's Slice costs 2 Void Shroud each. With 4 stacks, you can use it twice (2 + 2 = 4).

questions.scenario: You're level 92 and have used all your Void Shroud on Lemure's Slice.
questions.question: What additional finisher ability is available?
questions.explanation: Sacrificium (Lv.92) is an additional oGCD finisher that becomes available after spending Void Shroud during Enshroud.

questions.scenario: You have 3 Lemure Shroud remaining but Enshroud is about to expire.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: If Enshroud is expiring, use Communio to get the finisher damage rather than losing it entirely. Some damage is better than none.

questions.scenario: A fight just started. Your party has multiple raid buff jobs.
questions.question: When should you use Arcane Circle?
questions.explanation: Use Arcane Circle at the start of the fight to align with other party raid buffs. It's a 2-minute cooldown.

questions.scenario: Arcane Circle is active. All 8 party members are dealing damage.
questions.question: How many Immortal Sacrifice stacks can you gain?
questions.explanation: Each party member under Circle of Sacrifice can grant you 1 Immortal Sacrifice stack, for a maximum of 8 stacks.

questions.scenario: You have 8 Immortal Sacrifice stacks.
questions.question: How much Shroud Gauge does Plentiful Harvest grant?
questions.explanation: Plentiful Harvest consumes Immortal Sacrifice stacks and grants 50 Shroud Gauge, enabling quick Enshroud entry.

questions.scenario: It's been 2 minutes since the fight started. Arcane Circle is ready.
questions.question: What should you consider before using Arcane Circle?
options: Check if party buffs are ready
questions.explanation: Arcane Circle should align with party 2-minute buffs. Check if other raid buffs are being used for maximum benefit.

questions.scenario: Arcane Circle just ended. You have Immortal Sacrifice stacks.
questions.question: When should you use Plentiful Harvest?
questions.explanation: Use Plentiful Harvest before the Immortal Sacrifice buff expires. The Shroud gained helps enter Enshroud during buff windows.

questions.scenario: You're facing 4 enemies in a dungeon.
questions.question: Which combo should you use?
questions.explanation: At 3+ targets, use the AoE combo (Spinning Scythe → Nightmare Scythe) for more total damage.

questions.scenario: The boss jumped away and you can't reach it. You have Harvest Moon ready.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: Harvest Moon is a ranged GCD perfect for moments when you can't reach the boss. Use it during disengagement.

questions.scenario: You're waiting for a dungeon pull. Harvest Moon is not ready.
questions.question: What should you do?
options: Use Soulsow to prepare Harvest Moon
questions.explanation: Soulsow prepares Harvest Moon (5s cast, usable out of combat). Always enter fights with Harvest Moon ready.

questions.scenario: You're fighting 5 enemies. Death's Design isn't on any of them.
questions.question: What should you do first?
options: Apply Shadow of Death to each
questions.explanation: Whorl of Death applies Death's Design to all enemies in range. Apply it first for the +10% damage on all subsequent attacks.

questions.scenario: You have 2 enemies. One is nearly dead.
questions.question: Which rotation should you use?
options: Single-target on the low HP enemy
questions.explanation: Reaper's AoE threshold is 3+ targets. At 2 enemies, single-target rotation is more efficient.
